args.query: site:tesla.com support Supercharging payment balance unpaid amount due Tesla app

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Supercharging | Tesla Support

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/supercharging

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # Supercharging


  Tesla''s Supercharging support page covers charging locations, pricing, payment
  methods, invoices, unpaid balances and troubleshooting for Supercharger use.


  ## Payments and Fees


  Pricing for each Supercharger location is shown in the vehicle touchscreen and in
  the Tesla app. After a charging session, the vehicle can show an estimate of the
  final charges. Charging prices may vary by location and time, and some locations
  use different rates depending on demand.


  For Tesla vehicles, Supercharging charges are handled through the Tesla app. Before
  using a Supercharger, the account needs a payment method added and designated for
  Supercharging. Payments for Supercharging are then charged to that selected payment
  method.


  To manage a Supercharging payment method in the Tesla app:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu in the top-right corner.

  3. Tap **Charging**.

  4. Tap **Manage Payment**.

  5. Review or update the selected payment method and billing information.


  A primary payment method must remain on file for successful Supercharging purchases.
  The payment method and billing address can be updated from the same Charging payment
  area in the app.


  ## Unpaid Supercharging Balances


  An unpaid Supercharging balance appears in the Tesla app as a reminder that the
  balance is due. The unpaid balance is handled from the charging area of the app:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu in the top-right corner.

  3. Tap **Charging**.

  4. Tap **Pay Now**.


  Tesla advises users to keep both the billing address and payment method current
  before using a Supercharger. An unpaid balance or an incorrect payment method can
  prevent a vehicle from Supercharging.


  ## Supercharging Invoices


  Supercharging invoices are also managed from the Charging section of the Tesla app.
  Users can open **Charging History** to review past sessions and download invoices.


  ## Troubleshooting Supercharging Access


  Before trying to Supercharge, the account and vehicle setup should support the charging
  session. Tesla lists several basic prerequisites, including having the Tesla app,
  adding a payment method, designating a Supercharging payment method and ensuring
  a proper physical connection between the Supercharger and the vehicle.


  For vehicles purchased from a third party, the user must be listed as the vehicle
  owner before managing Supercharging payments.


  If a problem occurs before or during a charging session at a Supercharger, the Tesla
  app also provides a location-based **Report an Issue** function. Users select the
  charging location, choose the issue category and can include a photo. This reporting
  feature requires a current version of the Tesla app.

  '

title: Supercharging | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/supercharging

Your Supercharging access is blocked because there is an **unpaid Supercharging balance of $24.50**. There is no active vehicle-service or roadside-assistance process.

Handle it in the **Tesla app**:

1. Open the Tesla app.
2. Tap the menu in the top-right.
3. Select **Charging**.
4. Tap **Pay Now** and settle the $24.50 balance.
5. If needed, update your payment method under **Charging → Manage Payment**.

After the balance is paid, try starting the Supercharging session again. Tesla’s official guidance confirms that unpaid balances can prevent Supercharging: [Tesla Supercharging Support](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/supercharging).

final_answer:
Your Supercharging access is blocked because there is an **unpaid Supercharging balance of $24.50**. There is no active vehicle-service or roadside-assistance process.

Handle it in the **Tesla app**:

1. Open the Tesla app.
2. Tap the menu in the top-right.
3. Select **Charging**.
4. Tap **Pay Now** and settle the $24.50 balance.
5. If needed, update your payment method under **Charging → Manage Payment**.

After the balance is paid, try starting the Supercharging session again. Tesla’s official guidance confirms that unpaid balances can prevent Supercharging: [Tesla Supercharging Support](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/supercharging).
